title: Consolidate guard contract tests and close test gaps from task 208 review
analysis: Task 208 (project_id validation) was merged to main at c29fec6, adding: (1) a field_validator('project_id', mode='before') to Watermark that strips whitespace, (2) guard improvements in base.py with wm_pid extraction and debug logging for empty watermark, (3) TestWatermarkProjectIdValidator (5 tests) and TestBaseStageGuardContract (3 tests) in test_enum_models.py. However, a later merge conflict resolution (0ea23cb) accidentally dropped ALL of task 208's changes. The current codebase has the plain Watermark model (no validator), the simplified base.py guard (no debug logging), and neither test class. Task 215's review found that TestBaseStageGuardContract duplicates 3 tests already in TestProjectIdValidation (test_stages.py lines 405-582): matching project_id, empty watermark, and mismatch. Additionally two test gaps were identified: (a) no integration test for whitespace watermark project_id flowing through the validator→guard combined path, (b) no dict-construction test confirming mode='before' works for model_validate (DB deserialization). The fix: restore the validator and guard improvements, restore TestWatermarkProjectIdValidator but NOT TestBaseStageGuardContract, add the two missing tests.

prerequisites.description: Restore field_validator import and strip_project_id validator on Watermark model in reconciliation.py. Change import to 'from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator'. Add '@field_validator("project_id", mode="before") @classmethod def strip_project_id(cls, v): if isinstance(v, str): return v.strip(); return v' after the last field of Watermark.

Restore base.py guard improvements: extract wm_pid = watermark.project_id, use nested if for mismatch check, add else branch with logger.debug('Watermark has no project_id — skipping mismatch check (stage project_id=%r)', self.project_id). This replaces the single-line 'if watermark.project_id and watermark.project_id != self.project_id' with the structured version from task 208.

steps.description: Write TestWatermarkProjectIdValidator class in test_enum_models.py with 5 restored tests from task 208 plus 1 new dict-construction test (6 total). Restored tests: test_valid_project_id_passes_through_unchanged, test_whitespace_only_normalizes_to_empty_string, test_leading_trailing_whitespace_is_stripped, test_empty_string_passes_through_as_valid_sentinel, test_tabs_and_newlines_normalize_to_empty. New test: test_model_validate_dict_strips_whitespace — calls Watermark.model_validate({'project_id': '  dark_factory  '}) and asserts project_id == 'dark_factory', confirming mode='before' works for DB deserialization path. All 6 tests should FAIL because the field_validator is not yet restored.

Apply pre-1: restore field_validator import and strip_project_id on Watermark model. All 6 TestWatermarkProjectIdValidator tests now pass. Existing TestProjectIdValidation tests in test_stages.py continue to pass (empty watermark test relies on '' being falsy, which is preserved).

Add test_whitespace_watermark_project_id_treated_as_empty to TestProjectIdValidation in test_stages.py. Test creates Watermark(project_id='   '), passes through stage.run() with caplog at DEBUG level, asserts: (a) run succeeds (no ValueError), (b) result.stage is correct, (c) DEBUG log contains 'no project_id' or 'skipping' message. This tests the validator→guard combined path. Test should FAIL because base.py guard lacks the debug logging branch.

Apply pre-2: restore base.py guard improvements — extract wm_pid, add else-branch debug logging. The whitespace→empty integration test now passes: field_validator strips '   ' to '', guard sees falsy wm_pid, emits debug log, skips mismatch check. Run full test suite to confirm no regressions.

design_decisions.decision: Do NOT restore TestBaseStageGuardContract from task 208
design_decisions.rationale: All 3 tests (matching, empty, mismatch) are covered by TestProjectIdValidation in test_stages.py. The contract class duplicated both test logic and helper infrastructure (_patch_stage, mock_deps). TestProjectIdValidation has 6 tests that are a superset of the 3 contract tests.

design_decisions.decision: Restore TestWatermarkProjectIdValidator as a separate class in test_enum_models.py rather than moving to test_stages.py
design_decisions.rationale: test_enum_models.py tests Pydantic model/enum construction and coercion. The field_validator is model-level behavior (no stage logic). Keeping it in test_enum_models.py follows the existing pattern where each model's coercion tests live there (TestReconciliationRunCoercion, TestJudgeVerdictCoercion, etc.).

design_decisions.decision: Treat merge-conflict regression (0ea23cb) as prerequisite restoration, not as new feature development
design_decisions.rationale: Task 208 was properly reviewed and merged. The code loss was accidental during an unrelated merge conflict resolution. Restoring it is prerequisite setup, not new TDD work. The two NEW tests (dict-construction and whitespace→empty integration) are the actual TDD deliverables.

design_decisions.decision: Place whitespace→empty integration test in TestProjectIdValidation, not as a new class
design_decisions.rationale: TestProjectIdValidation already tests all BaseStage.run() project_id guard paths and has the _patch_stage helper infrastructure. The new test fits naturally as the 7th case in that class, testing the validator→guard combined path.

design_decisions.decision: Use model_validate({'project_id': ...}) not Watermark(project_id=...) for dict-construction test
design_decisions.rationale: model_validate() is the path used during DB deserialization (FalkorDB query results come as dicts). This exercises Pydantic's mode='before' validator pipeline for dict input, which is subtly different from keyword-arg construction and is the actual production path that needs coverage.

reuse.how: Reuse the existing _patch_stage context manager for the new whitespace→empty integration test. It patches assemble_payload and run_stage_via_cli to isolate the guard logic from expensive I/O.
Reuse the existing mock_deps fixture for the new integration test — provides AsyncMock memory_service, taskmaster, journal, and ReconciliationConfig.
Reuse the existing fake implementations for the new test — they return minimal valid responses so only the guard logic is exercised.
Restore the _make_watermark(project_id) helper for consistent Watermark construction in all validator tests.

reuse.what: Existing TestXxxCoercion class pattern in test_enum_models.py
reuse.how: Follow the same structure for TestWatermarkProjectIdValidator: private _make helper, individual test methods for each case, pytest.raises for error cases.
